calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions Or Alternatively Evidentiary And Or Issue Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-07-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JOHN SIMS' MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS, OR ALTERNATIVELY, EVIDENTIARY AND/OR ISSUE SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant John Sims' motion for terminating, evidentiary, and/or issue sanctions is granted in small part. Defendant did not need to meet and confer because this court's order was clear and there was nothing left to resolve. (Leach v. Superior Court (1980) 111 Cal.App.3d 902, 906.) Terminating sanctions are unwarranted since the plaintiff has now responded to the discovery and paid the sanctions. (Hultin Decl., pars. 8-10.) "[T]erminating sanctions are to be used sparingly, only when the trial court concludes that lesser sanctions would not bring about the compliance of the offending party." (R.S. Creative, Inc. v. Creative Cotton, Ltd. (1999) 75 Cal.App.4th 486, 496.) Plaintiff shall provide the verifications by the time of the hearing. Plaintiff shall pay $1,250 as reasonable sanctions because her failure to comply with this court's previous order lacked substantial justification. Payment to be made within 20 days of notice of entry of this order. Order signed. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/HEK)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE

MINI MINUTES: Casey Hultin, Esq., appearing for the plaintiff. The Order to Show Cause is re-set to April 07, 2026, at 10:30 am in Department 610. Judge: Rochelle East; Clerk Z. Dizon; Proceedings not reported. (610)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiff Susan Damrons Responses To Defendants Written Discovery And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-12-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JOHN SIMS'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff Susan Damrons Responses To Defendants Written Discovery And Request For Sanctions.

Defendant John Sims's motion to compel plaintiff Susan Damron to respond to his first set of form interrogatories, first set of special interrogatories, and first set of requests for production of documents (Treppa Decl., Exs. A-C) is granted. Damron has not responded (id., para. 9), and has thereby waived objections. (Code Civ. Proc., section 2030.290, subd. (a); id., section 2031.300, subd. (a).) Damron is ordered to respond within two weeks of notice of entry of this order. Damron is further ordered to pay $1500 to defendant within 30 days of notice of entry of this order. (Id., section 2030.290, subd. (c); id., section 2031.300, subd. (c); Rules of Court, Rule 3.1348, subd. (a).)

Proposed order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
